text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is printing money right now. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center gig runs **$53.80/hr** — that's 10% over scale on a 5-12 Monday-Friday grind plus 10-hour Saturdays. New construction, clean work, and they're burning through 25 hands. Industrial Electric's Eli Lilly LP1 in Lebanon is rotating Sunday with a 13-day rule and **$54.75 top scale**, but expect the heavy hours. Book is moving.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** has the crown jewel: Microsoft Mount Pleasant is stacked with four contractors—Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, Kelso Burnett—all running **$62.51/hr** flat. That's legit industrial scale for a data center build. Wisconsin inside JW or reciprocal only; long sleeves mandatory. 53 hands in the call; this one's hot.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is the per diem play. Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge Data Center pays **$44.80 base** but stacks **$100/day non-taxed per diem** (if you're 100+ miles out) plus incentive bonuses up to $150 on weekends. Six 10-hour days, 31.4% shift differential on the graveyard side. Sixty-two hands across three shifts; they're not slowing down.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** shows the data center arms race: four contractors (Gurtz, MJ Electric, Cupertino, Uihlein) all bidding Port Washington at **JW wage plus $10/hr**, cranking 50-60 hours weekly. Heavy, repetitive grind but the overtime math works.

**LU-379 Charlotte, NC** has three solid data center calls—Gross Electric (MOC-1, Spartanburg), Miller Electric (QTS, Rock Hill), Rosendin (MDN.06, Newton)—all running **$37–$40/hr** with per diem and night-shift premiums. Book depth is clean.

**Bottom line:** Data centers are vacuuming up hands coast-to-coast. If you can stomach 60-hour weeks and shift work, the money's there—especially in the upper Midwest and Mountain West where per diem stacks the take-home.
